mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero predictive power for 20-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances across a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion, increasing aggregate demand and equity risk premiums, which lifts S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 55% of rolling 60-day windows, indicating the interaction adds no incremental predictive information.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain resilience, which lowers input cost inflation and supports corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity returns such that positive INDPRO surprises consistently coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent triggers fears of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL prints above the threshold yet S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of cases, invalidating the inflation-valuation channel.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized combined with rising residential investment confirms robust aggregate demand, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, proving the proxies do not jointly predict equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect sustained business hiring, which directly correlates with rising corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS revisions consistently reverse the initial print direction, causing the signal to flip sign and produce negative predictive power for 5-day returns in over 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent signals a sharp contraction in residential construction activity, which drags down aggregate demand and corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Housing starts fall by the specified threshold while S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered episodes, refuting the demand transmission channel.
